measured_target.source: UConn Laminar Flame Speed Database compilation for iso-octane

reference_attempts.observed_runtime_note: Progress-tracked native Cantera chemistry-cache run completed mechanism load in about 98 s, restored the reduced warm-start profile in about 13 s, and then remained inside the first reference flame.solve stage for more than six minutes without a stage-completion event or offline cache.

reference_attempts.runtime_window_basis: Chem679 benchmark load and solve times were both materially slower than Chem323, so it was not advanced to the reference-quality cache attempt after Chem323 failed the runtime window.

decision_summary: Do not promote laminar flame speed into strict chemistry gating. The downloaded UConn workbook exposes a source-condition mismatch: the relevant iso-octane sheet is organized at 1 atm, 2 atm, and 3 atm, while the configured target is labeled 3.33 bar and does not match the 3 atm column mean. Chem323 also failed the reference-cache runtime window before this audit issue was resolved.
next_actions: Resolve the intended aggregation rule and pressure label for the UConn iso-octane flame-speed anchor.
Only rerun Chem323 reference-cache promotion after the flame-speed target definition is corrected.
Continue using ignition-delay chemistry authority for the current five-regime validation gate.
